goal: The agent reads and reasons over a OneDrive document within a single turn.
why: Operators expect single-turn document answers (e.g. summarize the latest invoice). Per phase-2-context.md the capability is ALREADY shipped (read_file + extractText, wired into registry + system prompt); the real deliverable is closing the verification + automated-test gap so it cannot silently regress. No redundant read_document tool is added (MVP/locality).
success_criteria: A tool fetches a drive item, extracts Word/PDF/Excel/text, and returns content the agent reasons over in the same turn.
Size/type guards: large files truncated with a note; unsupported types reported cleanly.
Wired into the tool registry and the system-prompt capability list.
Automated extraction test net exists and passes; tsc 0, full suite green.

tasks.title: Verify pre-existing document-read capability + add extraction test net
acceptance_criteria: read_file over a stubbed text download returns { fileName, content } with the decoded text.
When the download response has no content-disposition, the filename falls back to getItem(...).name.
An unsupported binary extension returns the clean 'Cannot extract text' message, not a crash.
Content longer than the 12000-char cap is truncated and ends with '(truncated)'.
Missing itemId returns { error: 'itemId is required' } with no download attempted.
.docx/.pdf/.xlsx filenames route to the matching parser branch, verified with the parser modules mocked.
npx tsc --noEmit exits 0; npx vitest run passes.
tasks.action: Add vi.mock for mammoth/pdf-parse/xlsx, a fileResponse helper, and a read_file describe block covering AC1-AC6 via executeTool('read_file', { itemId }, 'conn-1') with the download mock configured per case. Test OUR extraction dispatch, not the vendor libraries.
